governs the game of flat green outdoor [bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") for men and women in England. The organisation is responsible for the promotion and development of lawn bowls in England, and is affiliated with the world governing body [World Bowls](/wiki/World_Bowls "World Bowls").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Governing body of bowls in England

Bowls England

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Sport | [Bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") |
| Jurisdiction | England |
| Founded | 2008 |
| Affiliation | [World Bowls](/wiki/World_Bowls "World Bowls") |
| Location | Royal Leamington Spa |
| Chairman | Jo Doust |
| [CEO](/wiki/Chief_executive_officer "Chief executive officer") | Jon Cockcroft |
| Replaced | English Bowling Association and English Women’s Bowling Association |
| (founded) | 1903 |
| Official website | |
| [www.bowlsengland.com](https://www.bowlsengland.com/) | |
| [England](/wiki/England "England") | |

**Bowls England** governs the game of flat green outdoor [bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") for men and women in England. The organisation is responsible for the promotion and development of lawn bowls in England, and is affiliated with the world governing body [World Bowls](/wiki/World_Bowls "World Bowls").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bowls_England&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Bowls in England had been played for centuries, with the first documented evidence of a green having been in existence in Southampton during 1550, although the 'old green' is believed to have existed long before this.[[2]](#cite_note-GS-2) Despite such a long history, England failed to organise a national body before their [Scottish counterparts](/wiki/Bowls_Scotland "Bowls Scotland") and the first English reginal body was the 1882 Northumberland and Durham BA.[[2]](#cite_note-GS-2)

The Imperial Bowling Association was created in 1899, although this was also designed to assist the organisation of bowls in other countries and did not have permission to use the Scottish Association's rules. The legendary cricketer [W.G. Grace](/wiki/W.G._Grace "W.G. Grace") finally pulled the various county associations together on 8 June 1903 to form the English Bowling Association and Grace was elected its first president and the Scottish rules were adopted.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The [English national bowls championships](/wiki/English_national_bowls_championships "English national bowls championships") were inaugurated in 1905 and on 7 October 1931, the English Women's Bowling Association was formed at the York Hotel.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Bowls England was formed on 1 January 2008 following a merger of the English Bowling Association and the English Women's Bowling Association. The headquarters of Bowls England are located in Victoria Park, [Leamington Spa](/wiki/Leamington_Spa "Leamington Spa"), having moved from Worthing in 2013. The move was partly due to the fact that both the men's and women's national championships are held at [Victoria Park](/wiki/Victoria_Park,_Leamington_Spa "Victoria Park, Leamington Spa"), just five minutes walk away.

There are thirty-five affiliated county bowling associations, to which a total of 2,700 clubs are in membership nationwide. Approximately 100,000 players come under the jurisdiction of this association.

## Bowls players

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bowls_England&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Bowls players")]
